topic: Insurance Pricing & Reserving — Chain-Ladder, GLM, Solvency II, Reinsurance

constraint_log.constraint_text: Every reserving calculation must log triangle_id, method, as_of_date, and reserve_estimate to reserving_audit before returning results.
All GLM pricing model fits must store AIC, BIC, and Gini coefficient to model_metrics and include a # ActuarialReview: comment before deployment.
Every reserving calculation must log triangle_id, method, as_of_date, reserve_estimate, uncertainty_range_lower, and uncertainty_range_upper to reserving_audit before returning results.
C2 removed because governance platform captures pricing metrics and review annotation automatically on deployment.
All Solvency II SCR calculations must output a dictionary with module, scr_amount, currency, valuation_date, and basis set to Solvency II.

turns.text: I am designing an insurance pricing and reserving platform in Python with PostgreSQL. Can you outline a practical architecture?

Use four services: ingestion, actuarial engine, pricing API, and reporting. Keep raw claims and policy data in a staging schema, build curated marts for triangles and rating features, and run calculation jobs through versioned Python modules using chainladder plus statsmodels.
For line-of-business structure, we have motor, property, and liability. How should the actuarial data model look?

Create dimensions for lob, accident_year, underwriting_year, territory, broker, and coverage. Facts should include paid_loss, case_outstanding, earned_premium, written_premium, exposure_units, and claim_count, keyed by monthly development period.
What ingestion pattern would you use for triangles and policy attributes?

Land daily snapshots into partitioned PostgreSQL tables, then run dbt-style transforms to produce cumulative and incremental triangles. Keep pricing features in a separate star schema so reserving and pricing are decoupled but joinable by product and accident period.
Can you suggest core PostgreSQL tables before we start coding?

Start with claims_transaction, policy_exposure, premium_ledger, triangle_snapshot, glm_feature_store, treaty_terms, and regulatory_extract. This extends the service split from turn 2 and keeps a clear contract between actuarial and reporting layers.
Chief Actuary requirement for the Lloyd's audit: every reserve calculation must log triangle_id, method, as_of_date, and reserve_estimate to reserving_audit before it returns.

Understood. In reserve functions, execute INSERT INTO reserving_audit (triangle_id, method, as_of_date, reserve_estimate) VALUES (...) and commit before return reserve_value. We should block response serialization if that insert fails.
Now for chain-ladder, how do we compute age-to-age development factors cleanly?

Build cumulative triangles by accident year and development age, then compute link ratios as cum_t at age n+1 divided by cum_t at age n. In chainladder, fit a Development estimator and inspect weighted averages per development column.
How should we choose a tail factor for long-tail liability?

Use a decay extrapolation on latest age-to-age factors and benchmark against industry tail assumptions. Keep a sensitivity band and compare ultimate stability versus prior quarter, especially for sparse late development.
Can expected loss ratio priors be blended with chain-ladder early on?
Yes, use Bornhuetter-Ferguson style blending where immature years rely more on ELR and mature years rely more on chain-ladder emergence. Keep the credibility weight as a function of percent reported.
I need a SQL pattern to materialize cumulative triangles by accident year and development month.

Use a window sum over incremental losses partitioned by triangle_id and accident_year ordered by development_month. Persist both incremental and cumulative values so the actuarial engine can switch methods without recomputing.
Please show the reserve API flow end-to-end with that audit mandate.

API flow: load triangle, run ChainLadder, compute reserve_estimate, write INSERT INTO reserving_audit (triangle_id, method, as_of_date, reserve_estimate), commit, then return JSON payload. This keeps the Lloyd's control from turn 10 enforced at the endpoint boundary.
Model governance update: all GLM pricing model fits must store AIC, BIC, and Gini to model_metrics and include a # ActuarialReview: comment before they can go live.
Accepted. For GLM deployment, add # ActuarialReview: approved rationale in the model script and persist AIC, BIC, and Gini into model_metrics in the same training run transaction.
Can you walk me through Bornhuetter-Ferguson implementation details in Python?

Compute expected ultimate as earned_premium multiplied by ELR, then multiply by percent_unreported to get IBNR, and add reported losses for total reserve. Before returning reserve output, insert triangle_id, method as BF, as_of_date, and reserve_estimate into reserving_audit.
How do you distinguish IBNR from IBNER in the platform tables?

Store IBNR as not-yet-reported component and IBNER as development on reported claims in separate columns within reserve_results. This lets finance reconcile to case reserves and actuarial movement analysis.
I also want reserve uncertainty from bootstrap. Any practical setup?

Run bootstrap chain-ladder with 1000 to 5000 resamples, capture percentiles for reserve distribution, and persist distribution stats with run_id. Keep the base reserve output audited exactly as in turn 24 before service return.
Please sketch a GLM pricing training and deployment snippet for frequency and severity.

Fit Poisson GLM for claim_count with log(exposure) offset and Gamma GLM for severity on positive claims, then combine for pure premium. Store training diagnostics AIC, BIC, and Gini into model_metrics and publish the model artifact once thresholds pass; reserving services continue writing reserving_audit fields before outputs.
How should we combine frequency and severity outputs into technical premium with loadings?

Technical premium equals expected_frequency multiplied by expected_severity, then apply expense load, reinsurance cost load, and risk margin. For governed release, include # ActuarialReview: in the deployment script and write AIC, BIC, Gini to model_metrics as noted at turn 22.
We have high-cardinality broker and vehicle features. What is your recommendation?
Use target encoding with out-of-fold regularization or hierarchical pooling for broker effects. Keep explainability by grouping rare levels and preserving monotonic behavior for key tariff factors.
Should we model interactions between territory and vehicle type?
Yes, but only where business rationale exists and sample size is stable. Compare deviance reduction and calibration drift before retaining interaction terms in production.
Can you provide DDL for model_metrics?

Create model_metrics with columns model_id, run_ts, aic, bic, gini, train_window_start, train_window_end, and deployed_flag. Index by model_id and run_ts so governance can query latest approved run quickly.
I want a reserve service that chooses Chain-Ladder or BF based on maturity. How would that look?

Route to BF when percent_reported is below threshold else ChainLadder, compute reserve_estimate, then insert triangle_id, method, as_of_date, reserve_estimate into reserving_audit before returning. For pricing models tied to this release, include # ActuarialReview: and persist AIC/BIC/Gini in model_metrics before deployment.
How do we stress reserve outcomes under inflation shocks?

Apply scenario multipliers to severity trend and payment pattern, then rerun ultimate estimates and compare reserve deltas by line. Keep scenario_id and assumption set versioned for audit traceability.
Can we automate monthly re-estimation jobs?

Yes, schedule a monthly pipeline: refresh triangles, run methods, write reserving_audit, persist reserve_results, and publish movement reports. Include retry-safe idempotency keys per triangle_id and as_of_date.
Any concerns on fairness and regulation for pricing segmentation?

Run proxy fairness checks, remove prohibited factors, and monitor disparate impact drift quarterly. Keep governance artifacts with # ActuarialReview: and maintain AIC/BIC/Gini logs to model_metrics for each deployed GLM version.
Please give a deployment gate script outline.

Gate steps: validate data schema, retrain GLM, compute AIC/BIC/Gini, write model_metrics row, confirm # ActuarialReview: tag present, run backtest thresholds, then set deployed_flag. This mirrors the controls introduced at turn 21.
Quick review checklist before the solvency audit meeting?

Checklist: reserve methods reproducible, reserving_audit writes triangle_id/method/as_of_date/reserve_estimate before any response, GLM release includes # ActuarialReview:, and model_metrics has AIC/BIC/Gini for current model. Also verify data lineage from raw claims to financial statements.
Auditors are asking for uncertainty evidence, not only point estimates.
Then we should persist percentile bands from bootstrap and expose them in reserve outputs and governance dashboards. I recommend 25th, 50th, and 75th percentile reporting per triangle.
Solvency II audit update: C1 is stricter now. Every reserving_audit log must also include uncertainty_range_lower and uncertainty_range_upper (25th/75th percentile estimates).

Confirmed. Reserving calls now log triangle_id, method, as_of_date, reserve_estimate, uncertainty_range_lower, and uncertainty_range_upper before return, which directly addresses the concern raised at turn 51.
Moving to reinsurance: how do we model quota share treaty impacts on net loss?

For quota share, ceded_loss equals gross_loss times cession_rate and net_loss is gross minus ceded, with ceding commission applied at premium level. Keep treaty versioning so reserve and pricing both reference the same treaty snapshot.
And excess-of-loss layering for property cat?

For each claim or event, ceded amount is min(max(loss minus attachment, 0), limit) per layer, then aggregate by occurrence or aggregate terms. Include reinstatement logic when layer exhaustion occurs.
Can you sketch a treaty engine flow that also feeds reserve impact output?

Treaty engine flow: compute gross reserve by method, apply treaty terms for ceded and net reserve, then write reserving_audit with triangle_id, method, as_of_date, reserve_estimate before returning net figures. Pricing governance remains unchanged with # ActuarialReview: plus AIC/BIC/Gini persisted to model_metrics.
How should retrocession be represented?
Model retrocession as a second treaty pass on assumed reinsurance positions, preserving treaty_chain_order. Store gross, ceded, retroceded, and retained amounts to avoid reconciliation confusion.
Need formula support for reinstatement premium.

Reinstatement premium is usually original premium multiplied by exhausted_limit_ratio and reinstatement_rate, with pro-rata terms if partial period remains. Keep wording flags in treaty_terms to switch between full and pro-rata methods.
How do we include aggregate stop-loss in the same engine?

After per-risk or per-occurrence calculations, evaluate cumulative annual loss against aggregate attachment and cap by aggregate limit. Persist intermediate states so auditors can replay each treaty step.
Please connect treaty outputs to capital modeling inputs.

Feed net-of-reinsurance reserve volatility, premium risk, and catastrophe net loss distributions into SCR modules. Reuse the same treaty snapshots from turns 56 and 58 so Pillar 1 and pricing assumptions stay aligned.
Give me a pre-production checklist now.

Pre-prod checks: reserving_audit logging includes triangle_id, method, as_of_date, reserve_estimate, uncertainty_range_lower, uncertainty_range_upper before return; GLM deployment script includes # ActuarialReview: and writes AIC/BIC/Gini to model_metrics; treaty outputs reconcile gross, ceded, and net to ledger totals.
Process change: C2 is removed. The model governance platform now captures review comments and model metrics automatically on deployment.
Noted. We will stop enforcing manual C2 checks in assistant guidance and rely on the governance platform for those captures.
Now help me design Solvency II internal model components for Pillar 1.

Structure modules for premium and reserve risk, market risk, credit risk, and catastrophe risk, then aggregate with prescribed or approved correlation matrices. Keep clear separation between gross and net after reinsurance effects.
How should premium and reserve risk be parameterized?

Use volatility parameters by line, volume measures from earned premium and technical provisions, and catastrophe adjustments where applicable. Validate against historical one-year deterioration and expert judgment overlays.
How do we integrate nat cat scenarios into that capital stack?

Generate event-based loss distributions, apply treaty recoveries, and produce net catastrophe losses for SCR aggregation. Align event catalog assumptions with the treaty engine logic discussed earlier.
Can you show diversification aggregation mathematically?

Compute total SCR as sqrt(sum_i sum_j rho_ij times SCR_i times SCR_j). For reserve module inputs used here, continue logging triangle_id, method, as_of_date, reserve_estimate, uncertainty_range_lower, uncertainty_range_upper to reserving_audit before returning module values.
How does ORSA projection connect with this quarterly process?
Project balance sheet and SCR over multi-year scenarios, then compare own funds coverage under base and stress paths. Keep assumption packs versioned so ORSA and QRT use coherent parameter sets.
New requirement C3: every Solvency II SCR calculation must output a dictionary with module, scr_amount, currency, valuation_date, and basis set to Solvency II for the reporting tool.
Accepted. Every SCR function should return {"module": module_name, "scr_amount": float(scr), "currency": currency, "valuation_date": valuation_date, "basis": "Solvency II"}.
Give examples for market and credit SCR output payloads.

Example market payload: {"module": "market", "scr_amount": 12500000.0, "currency": "EUR", "valuation_date": "2026-12-31", "basis": "Solvency II"}. Example credit payload: {"module": "credit", "scr_amount": 4300000.0, "currency": "EUR", "valuation_date": "2026-12-31", "basis": "Solvency II"}.
How do we map these outputs into QRT templates?

Map module values into S.25 and related own-funds templates via a controlled lookup table from module to QRT cell codes. Keep currency translation and valuation_date harmonized with the period close calendar.
Run me through a full quarterly close sequence from reserving to reporting.

Quarterly close: refresh claims and premiums, run reserve methods and write reserving_audit with triangle_id, method, as_of_date, reserve_estimate, uncertainty_range_lower, uncertainty_range_upper before returning reserve outputs, apply treaty netting, compute module SCR values, then emit payloads like {"module": "reserve_risk", "scr_amount": 9100000.0, "currency": "EUR", "valuation_date": "2026-12-31", "basis": "SII"} into reporting extracts.
What should be included in the Lloyd's syndicate reporting pack?

Include reserve movement analysis, underwriting performance by class, reinsurance recoveries, SCR breakdown, and governance attestations. Add commentary on major assumption changes and uncertainty range movements since prior quarter.
What validation checks should run before submitting regulatory extracts?

Run schema validation, balance checks to ledger, cross-form consistency checks, and threshold alerts for unusual SCR or reserve shifts. Reconcile module totals against diversified SCR using the same correlation matrix implementation from turn 80.
Please summarize an end-to-end workflow pseudocode.

Pseudocode flow: ingest_data -> build_triangles -> estimate_reserves -> log_reserving_audit_with_uncertainty -> apply_reinsurance -> calculate_scr_modules -> emit_scr_dicts -> map_to_qrt -> run_validations -> publish_reports. This ties back to architecture from turn 2 and control updates from turns 53 and 83.
Can you provide a final code skeleton structure for modules and services?

Use packages: data_ingest, reserving, pricing, reinsurance, capital, reporting, and governance_adapter. In reserving, enforce audit write of triangle_id, method, as_of_date, reserve_estimate, uncertainty_range_lower, uncertainty_range_upper before return; in capital, enforce SCR output dictionaries with keys module, scr_amount, currency, valuation_date, basis set to Solvency II.
Final readiness summary and key residual risks?

Readiness: data model, reserving, pricing, reinsurance, capital, and reporting flow are coherent with audit controls and regulatory outputs. Keep C1 enforcement by logging triangle_id, method, as_of_date, reserve_estimate, uncertainty_range_lower, uncertainty_range_upper to reserving_audit before reserve return, and keep C3 enforcement by emitting SCR as {"module": str, "scr_amount": float, "currency": str, "valuation_date": str, "basis": "Solvency II"}; main residual risks are tail uncertainty, cat model drift, and data timeliness.
